message:
fix(db): classify lock errors as ResourceBusy and normalize archive agent names

Queries:
- map_sql_error() now checks if the error message indicates a database
  lock condition (via is_lock_error()) and maps those to DbError::ResourceBusy
  instead of the generic DbError::Sqlite. This allows callers to
  distinguish transient lock contention from permanent database errors
  and retry accordingly.
- Added tests verifying "database is locked" maps to ResourceBusy while
  other errors remain as Sqlite.

Reconstruct:
- Added normalized_archive_agent_name() that trims whitespace and
  rejects empty/blank agent names from archive JSONL files. This
  prevents reconstruction from creating agents with leading/trailing
  spaces or empty-string names.
- Updated parse_and_insert_message() to normalize the sender name
  through the new helper, falling back to "unknown" only when the
  normalized result is None.
- json_str_array() now trims each element and filters out blank
  entries, preventing empty-string recipients from being inserted.
- Updated existing tests to verify whitespace trimming and blank
  rejection behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
